name: Forward Health
tagline: AI-powered 'doctor in a box' CarePods for primary care
description: Forward raised $657M to build autonomous AI-powered CarePods for primary care. Only 5 of a planned 3,200 pods were deployed before the company abruptly shut down.
story: Founded in 2016 by former Google employee Adrian Aoun, Forward Health raised $657 million — including a $225 million Series D from SoftBank — to reinvent primary care with AI. The vision: autonomous CarePods staffed by AI instead of doctors, available for $99 per month. The reality fell far short. Forward burned through over $400 million while generating less than $100 million in total revenue. Of the 3,200 CarePods planned, only about 5 were ever deployed. Those that existed suffered sensor malfunctions and at least one incident of a patient getting trapped inside. In November 2024, Forward abruptly shut down, laying off approximately 200 employees. It was one of the most expensive failures in digital health history.
